run.title: Run analysis
run.noTargets: No workflows configured yet. Add one under Manage → Batch.
run.queueEmpty: Queue is empty — runs start right away
run.queueBusy: Queue full ({{n}} running) — {{ahead}} ahead of you, this will wait its turn.
queue.title: Run queue
queue.cancelRowConfirm: Cancel this row? A running row is aborted; a queued one is skipped.
queue.rescheduleHint: Pick a new time, or clear it to run as soon as possible.
queue.clearFinishedConfirm: Delete all finished and cancelled jobs?
chat.noTargets: No chat or agent apps yet. Add one under Manage → Targets (a Dify chat/agent app).
chat.enterHint: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a newline
chat.deleteConfirm: Delete this conversation? Dify keeps the messages.
apps.batchDesc: Run a workflow over many rows — see per-row results and re-run failures
apps.adminHint: Upload a .zip bundle (an app.json manifest + a self-contained index.html). Apps run in a sandboxed iframe and reach the API only through a scoped bridge. See docs/adr/0003-downloadable-apps.md.
apps.marketHint: One-click install community apps from the GitHub app market, or install manually by uploading a .zip bundle above.
apps.marketEmpty: The market has no apps to install yet.
apps.permNone: This app requests no data access.
batch.noTargets: No runnable targets yet. Ask an admin to configure a target under Manage → Batch jobs.
batch.admin.maxJobsHint: How many runs execute at once across the whole queue — a single analysis, or each row of a batch, is one run. 1 = one at a time, in priority order.
batch.admin.reservedSlotsHint: Slots kept for urgent jobs so they always have a lane (capped below the max).
batch.admin.ticketPeriodHint: How often each user's urgent tickets refill.
batch.ticketDowngraded: Out of urgent tickets — ran as normal
batch.csvPlaceholder: Paste CSV; first row is the header: {{keys}}
batch.countsFull: Total {{total}} · queued {{queued}} · running {{running}} · ok {{succeeded}} · partial {{partial}} · failed {{failed}}
batch.status.untrackedHint: The run was dispatched to Dify but left no trackable handle, so its final outcome can't be confirmed. It was not re-run, to avoid a duplicate charged run. If a run handle was kept, you can reconcile it manually later.
batch.msg.clamped: Concurrency capped to {{n}} by the admin limit
batch.admin.maxConcurrencyHint: An operator's chosen concurrency will not exceed this.
batch.admin.urgentEnabledHint: Off by default. When off, the urgent lane is hidden everywhere and any urgent submit runs at its base priority.
batch.admin.difyEndUserHint: The `user` Dify records for each run. Use [username] for the submitting portal user, e.g. [username]@anchan.kazuha.org. Blank = report-portal.
batch.admin.defaultPriorityHint: Base priority (0–100) for a run whose submitter is in no group with a default. Urgent runs still require a ticket.
batch.admin.prioWeightsHint: A queued run's score = w_base·base + w_age·age + w_fair·fair (each factor 0–1). Higher weight makes that factor matter more; 0 disables it.
batch.admin.wBaseHint: Weight of the configured base priority.
batch.admin.wFairHint: Weight of fair-share — heavy recent users yield to lighter ones.
batch.admin.ageHoursHint: Wait time at which the age factor reaches its max (1).
batch.admin.fairHalflifeHint: A user's recent runs decay by half over this window.
batch.admin.advancedPluginsHint: Import a manifest to run a non-Dify provider. Most setups only need Dify targets above.
batch.admin.difyPollHint: 0 = keep the streaming connection open until the run finishes. Set >0 to instead grab the run id then poll its status every N seconds — avoids a long-held stream through a proxy.
batch.dify.chatHint: This is a chat / agent app — each row's `query` column is the message it runs.
batch.dify.targetsHint: No targets yet. Add a Dify workflow with its API key and its inputs are discovered automatically.
batch.dify.baseUrlHint: e.g. https://your-dify/v1 — the service API base, ending in /v1.
batch.dify.apiKeyHint: The app-… service key of the workflow to run. One key per workflow.
batch.dify.apiKeyKeepHint: Leave blank to keep the current key. Paste a new key to replace it.
batch.dify.apiKeyKeepPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the current key
batch.dify.connectedNoInputs: Connected to "{{name}}", but inputs could not be read. Add them manually below.
batch.dify.inputsManual: Inputs could not be read automatically — add them manually.
batch.dify.noInputs: No inputs — add at least one.
webhook.title: Event webhooks
webhook.hint: On new reports or finished batch jobs, a signed event is POSTed to these URLs. Write the receiver in your own service (push to Feishu / send email / …).
webhook.secretHint: Optional. Used for the HMAC-SHA256 signature so subscribers can verify authenticity.
webhook.required: URL and at least one event are required
login.title: Sign in
home.searchPlaceholder: Type a code or name — pick a suggestion, or Enter to search all…
reader.title: Reading settings
common.name: Name
manage.title: Administration
links.label: Button text
links.hint: Drag to reorder; drop a link under a group header to put it in that group.
links.deleteGroupConfirm: Delete this group? Its links move back to the top level.
types.label: Display name (tab)
types.reclassifyConfirm: Reclassify all stored reports by the current category mapping?
types.restoreDefaultsConfirm: Clear all current type config and restore the defaults auto-generated on first run? Custom types are removed; stored reports are unaffected (a type that still has reports stays on as uncategorized).
users.weightHint: How many urgent runs each member may make per period. 0 = none.
users.urgentUnlimitedHint: Members of this group can run urgent jobs without spending tickets. Admins are unlimited only when they belong to such a group.
users.priorityHint: Base priority (0–100) a member's runs default to. Empty inherits the system default. Urgent runs are separate and require a ticket.
users.primaryGroupHint: Leave empty to inherit the Default group.
users.defaultGroupHint: Every user without an assigned group inherits these settings.
users.allowUrgentHint: May members use the urgent lane at all. Off blocks urgent for this group even when the lane is enabled.
users.maxQueuedHint: Most runs a member may have queued or running at once. 0 = unlimited. Admins are exempt.
users.runWindowHint: Hours (panel time) when members may start runs, e.g. 9-18, or 22-6 to wrap midnight. Empty = any time. Admins are exempt.
users.urgentPolicyHint: Whether members may use the urgent lane, and whether it costs a ticket.
settings.siteTitleTooLong: Site title must be 80 characters or fewer
settings.logoHint: Use an http(s) image URL, a site path starting with /, or upload a small image under 512KB. Uploaded files are saved as site file paths. Leave blank to use the default icon.
settings.logoReadFailed: Failed to read the logo file
settings.pwaIconHint: Use an http(s) image URL, a site path starting with /, or upload a small image under 512KB. Blank = follow the logo; if the logo is blank, use the default icon.
settings.announcementPopup: Show as popup when visiting Home
settings.announcementTitlePlaceholder: Example: Some nodes had auth errors (fixed, monitoring)
settings.announcementTitleTooLong: Announcement title must be 160 characters or fewer
settings.announcementContentPlaceholder: Example: We have deployed a fix and are monitoring the situation. If access still fails, try switching to another node.
settings.announcementContentTooLong: Announcement content must be 2000 characters or fewer
settings.announcementHint: The banner stays visible and cannot be dismissed. The popup appears whenever Home is visited; clicking "Don't show again" stores the dismissal in the current browser. Editing the title, content, or level shows it again.
settings.footerTextHint: Supports safe lightweight HTML such as a, strong, and br. Blank = use the site title.
settings.footerTextTooLong: Footer info must be 1000 characters or fewer
settings.homeMoreStyleHint: How folded quick links reveal when “More” is clicked.
settings.timezoneHint: Sets the timezone for the server-side notion of "today" (date=today, /api/v1/now) and non-browser rendering (PDF/email). Blank = follow the system zone. Timestamps in the UI render in each viewer's own browser timezone; report trading dates are market dates, identical for everyone.
login.forgotHint: Enter your username or email. If the account exists, we'll send a reset link.
login.forgotSent: If that account exists, a reset link is on its way — check your email.
reset.title: Set a new password
reset.noToken: This reset link is missing its token.
reset.error: Could not reset the password. The link may have expired.
email.hint: SMTP for password reset and run-done notifications. These settings live in the database, not config.yaml.
email.passKeep: Leave blank to keep the saved password
email.publicUrlHint: e.g. https://portal.example.com — used to build reset links. Set this so links can't be poisoned via forged request headers.
chat.busy: The assistant is busy — too many chats in progress. Try again in a moment.
chatAdmin.limitHint: Across all users; over this, new turns are asked to retry. 0 = unlimited. Chat is interactive and runs independently of the run queue.
batch.admin.difyRunTimeoutHint: Max time to wait/poll for one run before giving up. Must exceed your longest workflow — a run cut short is wrongly marked failed. Default 180.
queue.reconcileConfirm: Fetch this run's true outcome from Dify without re-running it?
queue.reconcileStillRunning: Still running on Dify — left unchanged
preset.label: Name
preset.overrunHint: What to do with a run still waiting when the window closes; a run already in progress always finishes.
batch.admin.runDefaultModeHint: Which button the run dialog opens on.
batch.admin.presetsHint: Users can pick one of these when running; the run is scheduled into that window.
preset.invertHint: When on, a run using this preset only starts OUTSIDE these windows — never during them. A run already in progress is unaffected and finishes.
chatAdmin.convsHint: Read-only oversight of every user’s assistant chats.
chatAdmin.streamHint: Off: the reply returns all at once (fallback if streaming misbehaves behind a proxy).
chatAdmin.reconcileHint: How long to actively poll Dify for a dropped turn’s result. A turn still running past this is NOT marked failed — it’s picked up when you reopen the chat (default = the per-turn limit).
chat.stillRunning: Still generating — this can take a while; it updates when it finishes.
storage.title: Cleanup & retention
storage.scheduleHint: Run the enabled targets automatically on this cadence.
storage.batchHint: Delete finished/cancelled runs older than the retention.
storage.tokensHint: Delete tokens expired longer ago than the grace period.
storage.reportsWarn: Only reports with a real ingest timestamp older than the retention are removed; imported / date-only reports are always kept. Off by default.
storage.confirmReportsBody: {{count}} report(s) older than {{days}} days match and will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
storage.confirmBody: Permanently delete {{n}} {{cat}} older than {{days}} days. This cannot be undone.
update.title: New version available
update.desc: The app was updated. Refresh to load the latest version.
apps.recurringDesc: Run a workflow on a repeating schedule — daily, weekly, or monthly.
recurring.title: Scheduled Tasks
recurring.intro: Save a workflow + inputs and let the portal run it on a repeating schedule. Each firing enters the same run queue.
recurring.noTargets: No run targets yet — create one in the batch console first.
recurring.errRows: Pick a target and enter at least one row.
recurring.runNowBody: This fires the task immediately, without affecting its schedule.
recurring.deleteBody: The schedule and its fire history are removed. Runs it already created are kept.
recurring.priorityIdleLong: Idle (run only when the queue is free)
recurring.tzHint: Fires in the panel timezone. A missed period (server offline) is skipped, not backfilled.
recurring.targetDeletedWarn: The original target was deleted — pick a new one before saving.
queue.truncatedHint: Showing the {{shown}} most recent of {{total}} jobs — older finished jobs are hidden; prune them in Storage cleanup.
chatAdmin.turnTimeoutHint: Longest one reply may run before the live stream gives up — raise it for long agent / deep-research replies. (Behind a reverse proxy, raise nginx proxy_read_timeout to match.)
